tasks.title: Confirm Vietnam entry eligibility
tasks.description: Check whether your passport is visa-exempt or requires an e-visa before travel.

tasks.title: Prepare immigration documents
tasks.description: Carry passport, onward ticket, and accommodation confirmation for arrival screening. Remember to download or screenshot your visa for easy access on arrival day.

tasks.title: Arriving in Vietnam
tasks.description: Arrival immigration and customs can take 2-3 hours, depending on timing, and e-passport processing is limited. Paid fast-track is available for a shorter queue, but wait times still vary widely. If you choose fast-track, expect anything from about 10 to 45 minutes.

tasks.title: Choose and Set Up eSIM
tasks.description: Compare eSIM options before you buy (estimated pricing).

tasks.title: Paying for things: Cash or Card or other
tasks.description:
Most places accept contactless card payments (about 80%), and many call it Apple Pay.
- Keep some cash for smaller shops and local spots.
- ATM withdrawals are often capped at around 3,000,000 VND per withdrawal, but limits vary by bank.
- Card providers like UP, ING, Ubank, Wise, and Revolut can differ on ATM fee refunds and FX rates, so check your account terms first.
- Backup option: transfer to my PayID and I can withdraw from my Vietnam account.
- QR payments are common via local bank apps; Moreta is another option people recommend across parts of Southeast Asia (I have not used it yet).

tasks.title: Pick Ride-Share Apps
tasks.description: Pick 1 or 2 ride-share apps before arrival for airport transfers and city navigation. Most apps can link to a local bank account or local e-wallet for smooth cashless payments (while still supporting cash in many cases).

rideShareApps.keyAdvantage: Fastest pickup times in almost any city/alleyway.
Very clean cars; professional drivers; no cancellations.
Best local promotions; integrates flight & bus tickets.

tasks.title: Review Public Transport Options
tasks.description:
Ho Chi Minh City now has one operating metro line: Metro Line 1 (Ben Thanh - Suoi Tien Terminal).
- Typical service window: around 05:00-22:00.
- Typical frequency: around 8 min peak / 12 min off-peak.
- Typical single-fare range: around 7,000-20,000 VND (distance based).
- Suggested stations for most visitors: Ben Thanh, Opera House, Ba Son, Thao Dien, Suoi Tien Terminal.
- Save your station exits and one backup bus/ride-hail route in case your final stop is not walkable.

tasks.title: Ho Chi Minh City weather packing
tasks.description:
Ho Chi Minh City is hot and humid year-round, so pack lightweight breathable clothing, hydration basics, and sun protection.
- In the rainy season (May-November), add a compact rain layer and quick-dry footwear.
- In the dry season (December-April), prioritize heat and sun comfort.

tasks.title: Neighbourhood and Stay Guidance
tasks.description:
Ho Chi Minh City neighbourhood quick guide:
- District 1: Great for hotels across luxury and budget options, plus plenty of Airbnbs. Most central for first-timers, but often louder and pricier.
- District 3: Mainly smaller hotels and Airbnbs, with greener and more local streets while still close to central areas.
- District 2: Mainly Airbnbs, with a more residential/expat-friendly feel and international amenities, but typically longer commutes.
- District 5 (Chinatown): Strong local character and food culture, usually better value, with a denser traditional feel.
Choose based on commute time, budget, and late-night noise tolerance.

tasks.title: Plan return trip to airport
tasks.description: Book or plan transport to the airport with enough buffer for traffic and departure procedures.
